message:
perf(query): always one-time materialize non-correlated IN subqueries, plus EXISTS direct-probe validation matrix

connection.rs (B2.1):
- Unconditionally one-time materialize all non-correlated IN
  subqueries by removing the needs_eager guard. Previously, plain
  non-correlated IN subqueries like WHERE id IN (SELECT aid FROM
  big_table) were left as InSet::Subquery and re-evaluated per
  outer row, causing O(N*M) behavior. Now the subquery is executed
  once, its results collected into an InSet::List of literals,
  and the existing HashSet probe path handles the rest in O(N+M).
- Removes the now-unnecessary in_subquery_needs_eager_eval and
  in_subquery_references_view checks since all non-correlated
  subqueries are always materialized

in_exists_regression.rs (B2 EXISTS validation matrix):
- E1: simple single-table correlated EXISTS with equality
  (500 orders, 100 customers)
- E2: correlated EXISTS with equality + static range filter
  (200 products, 50 categories, range c.id <= 25)
- E3: NOT EXISTS returns complement set (50 odd parents with
  no children)
- E4: multi-column correlated EXISTS (2-column equality match)
- E-null: correlated EXISTS where outer column is NULL
  (verifies NULL comparison returns FALSE)
- F1: EXISTS with JOIN in inner query (3-table chain)
- F2: EXISTS with GROUP BY + HAVING in inner query (aggregate
  threshold filter)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
